reason: Detached lineage keeps graph telemetry local; max-iterations forbids early synthesis.

focus: Correctness: phase topology authority and parent-to-child execution path

findingDetails.title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology
findingDetails.claim: The parent specification presents a current 16-phase 000–015 execution topology that conflicts with the authoritative re-decomposed phase tree and current terminal phase sequence.
findingDetails.counterevidence: The 16-phase map may have been retained as historical planning context, but it is presented as the parent program's current execution map and contains no historical-only boundary.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The parent map intentionally preserves an earlier decomposition for provenance. That remains unsafe while it is labelled as the active phase documentation map and conflicts with the authoritative manifest.
findingDetails.recommendation: Regenerate the parent frontmatter and phase map from manifest/phase-tree.json, or clearly archive the old map outside the active execution contract.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The mismatch is confined to packet documentation topology; no implementation target was modified.

results.evidence: F001: active parent topology conflicts with the authoritative phase tree.
No implementation checklist evidence is asserted in this topology pass.

reason: Multiple current execution documents directly contradict the active parent phase map.
F001 has direct evidence, counterevidence, an alternative explanation, and a remediation path.
Zero new findings are telemetry only; broaden to traceability because max-iterations requires all five passes.

focus: Security: destructive-operation, reference-resolution, and symlink safety controls

results.evidence: Guard and tooling specifications require dry-run/read-only controls, exemption handling, and explicit failure conditions.
005.002 checklist requires zero-scan failure, non-execution, no symlink-directory traversal, and no index mutation.

reason: No new security claim was raised; the active P1 remains independently evidenced and open.
Traceability identified new evidence; continue to the required maintainability pass.

focus: Traceability: phase-000 bootstrap prerequisites and evidence ownership

findingDetails.title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology
findingDetails.claim: The active parent topology remains in conflict with the authoritative phase tree.
findingDetails.recommendation: Regenerate the parent phase map from the authoritative phase tree.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

findingDetails.title: Phase 000 requires unavailable predecessor and rename-map evidence
findingDetails.claim: Phase 000 cannot satisfy its own blocking checklist because it is the first phase, while the checklist requires landed predecessors and a rename-map hash owned by a later phase.
findingDetails.counterevidence: The checklist may be a reusable SOL template, but it calls itself the blocking phase-000 verifier contract and has not marked the prerequisites as conditional or not applicable.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The program may intend the map hash to be absent at bootstrap and added later. The present wording still makes it a pre-commit obligation for phase 000.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make CHK-007 explicitly assert no predecessor for phase 000, remove or mark the rename-map hash as not applicable there, and require that hash only from the phase that freezes the map onward.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The contradiction is within phase-000 planning and verification documents; no target artifact was changed.

results.evidence: F002: phase-000 scope and checklist require evidence that phase ordering makes unavailable.
CHK-007 and CHK-008 conflict with phase-000 adjacency and scope.

reason: Phase adjacency, scope, and checklist statements directly conflict.
F001 and F002 both carry direct evidence, counterevidence, an alternative explanation, and remediations.
All named review dimensions are now covered; max-iterations requires a fifth pass, broadened to lifecycle/worktree alignment.

focus: Maintainability: duplicated parent phase maps and authoritative navigation

findingDetails.title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology

findingDetails.title: Phase 000 requires unavailable predecessor and rename-map evidence
findingDetails.claim: Phase 000 cannot satisfy its own blocking checklist because it is the first phase, while the checklist requires landed predecessors and a rename-map hash owned by a later phase.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make phase-000 evidence phase-local and defer rename-map hashing to phase 006 onward.

findingDetails.title: Duplicate partial phase map competes with the packet topology
findingDetails.claim: The second parent phase documentation map presents only phases 009–011 as the aggregate progress map, creating a competing and incomplete navigation contract.
findingDetails.counterevidence: The second map could be a narrow final-phase handoff view, but it calls itself the parent phase documentation map and aggregate progress source rather than a scoped appendix.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The partial table was left by a template scaffold. That still creates a stale source of truth because its transition rules apply it to the whole packet.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove the duplicate map or generate one unambiguous map from the canonical graph/manifest; keep any final-phase view explicitly scoped and non-authoritative.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The issue is limited to parent documentation maintainability; no production or target artifact was changed.

results.evidence: F003: a second phase map conflicts with graph metadata as the usable navigation source.
This pass assessed parent-map maintainability rather than a phase implementation checklist.

findingDetails.title: Phase 000 hard-codes a non-allocating legacy worktree identity
findingDetails.claim: Phase 000 directs creation of a literal legacy wt/ branch and numbered worktree instead of using the current clone-wide allocator and owner-first worktree contract.
findingDetails.counterevidence: The literal path may preserve a historical allocation, but phase 000 is planned work and does not record a valid allocation or identify the branch as an existing legacy exception.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The packet may have been authored before owner-first allocation became the project contract. Its current planned status means the stale instruction would still direct a new nonconformant worktree creation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace the literal path and wt/ ref with the sk-git allocator invocation, then record the returned owner-first ref and path in the baseline evidence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The mismatch is between planned packet documentation and the current repository worktree workflow; no worktree was created during review.

results.evidence: F004: phase-000 worktree creation is not aligned with the active sk-git allocator contract.
The bootstrap verification plan cannot prove allocator ownership because it uses a literal legacy ref.
No implementation consumer exists yet; this pass assessed the documented lifecycle contract.
The active sk-git skill documents an allocator and owner-first workflow; the packet should use it.

reason: The planned worktree creation command directly conflicts with the current required allocation workflow.
All three P1 findings carry direct evidence, counterevidence, alternate explanations, and targeted remediations.
